description:
You are working as a DevSecOps engineer who must strictly enforce “policy-as-code” around semantic versioning and changelogs for a small internal tool that lives in  
    /home/user/project  

    CURRENT STATE OF THE REPO  
    1. /home/user/project/VERSION  
       • Contains exactly the single line “1.2.3” (no extra spaces or newline at the top).  
    2. /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md  
       • Its first two lines are:  
         ## [1.2.3] - 2023-11-01  
         ### Fixed  
         (followed by one bullet that is not relevant to this task).  
    3. /home/user/project/scripts/bump_patch.sh  
       • An already-provided helper that takes **one** required argument (the changelog line), bumps the patch number, rewrites VERSION, and prepends a section to CHANGELOG.md.  
       • It also writes a one-line summary (“Bumped to <new-version>”) to STDOUT.  
       • It never needs sudo.

    POLICY YOU MUST ENFORCE  
    • Only a patch bump is allowed when the public API hasn’t changed.  
    • Every bump must be accompanied by an itemised changelog entry describing the fix.  
    • All changelog entries must follow the exact format below (the automated grader checks this verbatim!):

      Line 1: “## [NEW_VERSION] - 2024-01-15”  
      Line 2: “### Fixed”  
      Line 3: “- resolve false positive in dependency scanning (#42)”

      A single blank line must appear **after** the third line, after which the previous 1.2.3 section must remain untouched.

    YOUR TASK  
    Using one single terminal command, do ALL of the following:

    1. Invoke /home/user/project/scripts/bump_patch.sh with the precise changelog text
          resolve false positive in dependency scanning (#42)
       (Quotation marks as required by the shell are allowed but the inner text must not vary.)

    2. Capture the script’s STDOUT into /home/user/project/bump.log.  
       • The file must end with a newline and contain exactly the single line:
         Bumped to 1.2.4

    EXPECTED END-STATE (what the automated tests will look for)  
    • /home/user/project/VERSION now contains only “1.2.4”.  
    • /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md starts with the three-line section shown above, followed by a blank line, and then the original 1.2.3 section exactly as it was.  
    • /home/user/project/bump.log exists and contains the exact one-line string indicated.

    IMPORTANT  
    • Do NOT manually edit files; use the helper script.  
    • Keep everything within /home/user/project.  
    • Remember, you have to accomplish the entire task with just **one** shell command.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:

    1. File: /home/user/project/VERSION  
       Content (with terminating newline):  
       1.2.3⏎

    2. File: /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md  
       Content (with terminating newline):  
       ## [1.2.3] - 2023-11-01⏎
       ### Fixed⏎
       - initial release⏎

    3. File: /home/user/project/scripts/bump_patch.sh  
       Permissions: 0755  
       Simplified behaviour:  
         • Reads old version from ../VERSION.  
         • Increments the patch number.  
         • Overwrites ../VERSION with new version.  
         • Prepends to ../CHANGELOG.md the three-line header plus a blank line and keeps previous content.  
         • Echoes “Bumped to <new-version>” to STDOUT.

    After correct execution, expected state:

    1. /home/user/project/VERSION  
       Content:  
       1.2.4⏎

    2. /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md  
       Content (exact, including blank lines):  
       ## [1.2.4] - 2024-01-15⏎
       ### Fixed⏎
       - resolve false positive in dependency scanning (#42)⏎
       ⏎
       ## [1.2.3] - 2023-11-01⏎
       ### Fixed⏎
       - initial release⏎

    3. /home/user/project/bump.log  
       Content:  
       Bumped to 1.2.4⏎
